name: watercress

supplementary_research.finding: Randomized crossover trial (n=60 healthy adults, 85g raw watercress daily for 8 weeks): basal DNA damage in lymphocytes reduced by 17% (P=0.03) and oxidative purine DNA damage reduced by 23.9% (P=0.002); plasma lutein increased 100% and beta-carotene 33%. Protective effects more pronounced in smokers. Suggests cancer risk reduction via DNA protection and antioxidant elevation.

Narrative review (PMID 34055006): watercress extract upregulates superoxide dismutase and catalase, reduces malondialdehyde; in hypercholesterolemic models, lowers LDL-C by 48–52%, triglycerides by 30–44%, raises HDL-C; hepatoprotective and renoprotective effects confirmed across multiple models; anti-cancer activity spans initiation, proliferation, and metastasis stages via PEITC-mediated apoptosis and cell cycle arrest.

2025 narrative review classifies watercress as a functional food for non-communicable disease prevention; identifies glucosinolates and isothiocyanates (especially PEITC) as primary bioactive drivers of anti-inflammatory, antiproliferative, and metabolic regulatory effects relevant to cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and cancer risk reduction.

Ranked #1 on the US CDC Powerhouse Fruits and Vegetables list; contains over 40 unique flavonoids; cruciferous vegetable family membership associated with 16% reduced risk of heart disease in epidemiological data; high vitamin K content (>100% RDI per cup) linked to 35% lower hip fracture risk.

culinary_pairings.source: Great British Chefs – classic watercress and orange salad
Food and Travel Magazine – smoked fish and watercress pairings
Flavonomics – watercress, walnut and blue cheese salad tradition

seasonality: Spring and autumn peak; available year-round from aquatic farms

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Fat-soluble; absorption improved with dietary fat. High intake may interact with warfarin anticoagulation therapy.
Released by chewing or chopping; heat-sensitive – raw consumption preserves levels. Primary driver of anti-cancer and antioxidant effects.
Carotenoids absorbed with fat; clinical trial showed 100% plasma lutein increase after 8 weeks daily watercress consumption. Supports macular and cognitive health.
Water-soluble; destroyed by prolonged cooking. Raw or lightly wilted preparations preserve most content.

synergies.note: Fat from olive oil enhances absorption of fat-soluble compounds in watercress (vitamin K, carotenoids, PEITC). A drizzle of olive oil in salads or wilted preparations maximises bioavailability.

Both are glucosinolate-rich cruciferous vegetables; combined intake broadens the spectrum of isothiocyanates (PEITC from watercress, sulforaphane from broccoli), potentially additive in Nrf2 pathway activation and phase II detoxification enzyme induction.

Vitamin C in citrus stabilises watercress carotenoids and enhances non-haem iron absorption from watercress. Lemon juice also brightens the peppery flavour and is a standard pairing in both raw salads and cooked applications.
